en.extract:
The forest rock thrush (Monticola sharpei) is a songbird in the family Muscicapidae, formerly placed in the Turdidae together with the other chats. It now includes Benson's rock thrush and  Amber Mountain rock thrush as subspecies.
It is endemic to Madagascar. The type locality is the forests east of Ambatondrazaka, which in turn lies south of Lake Alaotra.
Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest and subtropical or tropical moist montane forest.

It now includes [[Benson's rock thrush]] and [[Amber Mountain rock thrush]] as [[subspecies]]. It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to [[Madagascar]]. The [[type locality (biology)|type locality]] is the forests east of [[Ambatondrazaka]], which in turn lies south of [[Lake Alaotra]]. Its natural [[habitat]]s are [[subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest]] and subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]]. ==Gallery== Amber mountain rock thrush (Monticola sharpei erythronotus) male 2.jpg|male Amber mountain rock thrush
